ead.archdesc:
======= RMA01416Cornell University College of Architecture, Art and Planning student projects15-5-1416Compiled by Laura LinkeDivision of Rare and Manuscript Collections
May 20082B Carl A. Kroch LibraryCornell UniversityIthaca, NY 14853rareref@cornell.eduURL:

converted from EAD 1.0 to EAD 2002 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Cornell University College of Architecture, Art and Planning student projects
Cornell University. College of Architecture, Art, and Planning
Murdock, Richard C.
Root, John Wellborn, 1887-1963.
Eidlitz, Robert James, 1864-1935.
Tissington, Richard Andrews.
DeLong, Samuel Richmond.
Carrère & Hastings
15-5-1416
29 cubic feet. 29 cubic feet.
1898-2006. Reports, Research Materials, Scrapbooks Volumes containing honors designs and theses; an interscholastic competition; and merit solutions for landscape design, also, student projects and drawings, and photographs of projects. Collection material in English <<<<<<< HEAD 44 41 42 43 44 26 - folder 1 ======= 44 41 42 43 44 26 - folder 1 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab 2080085
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
Laura Linke
Date completed:
May 2006
EAD encoding:
RMC Staff, May 2008
Date modified:
RMC Staff, March 2016
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Volumes containing honors designs and theses; an interscholastic competition; and merit solutions for landscape design, 1912-1922. Also, student projects and drawings, and photographs of projects, 1903-1967; three banners and one plaque for the Inter College Athletic Championships, 1910-1916; approximately 300 student pen and ink sketches, primarily copied from published works; and student projects for the Dept. of City and Regional Planning. Includes drawings of Cornell University and Ithaca buildings, such as Sage College, Boardman and Cascadilla Halls, Llenroc, the Grant Mansion, Giles House and student apartments, as well as proposed residential halls and academic buildings. Also, drawings of historic European and classical structures, and architectural elements. Among the students and architects whose work is represented are Carráere & Hastings, Richard C. Murdock (Cornell University Class of 1927), John Wellborn Root (Cornell University Class of 1909), and Robert J. Eidlitz (Cornell University Class of 1885).
Includes design problems, 1922-1954.
Includes Baird Prize competition "Place Plate" artwork from Design 112, February 1932. The winning design (not included) was used by the Wedgwood Company for a set of twelve plates depicting Cornell scenes.
Drawing on canvas by Richard Andrews Tissington (written RA Tissington), who graduated from the College of Architecture in 1900. According to a 1901 publication of Cornell Alumni News, the drawing, entitled "Design for a Ceiling," was the First Prize Drawing in the Cornell Traveling Fellowship Competition. It was also featured in a exhibit for the Convention of the Architectural League of America May 23-25, 1901. The drawing itself is approximately 48"x60"and is extremely fragile.
Framed rendering of a design for an "Alumni Hall - East Elevation. Cornell University" created as a student project by Samuel R. DeLong, Class of 1895, Architecture.
A 2000 "Praxis2" booklet compiled as the final report of a collaborative Architecture/Planning Course working with the Ithaca Neighborhood Housing Services on a site design for a housing development on West Hill in Ithaca, N.Y.
SERIES LIST
Series I. Miscellaneous Projects 1885 - 1997
Box 1-6, 35-39
Series II. Theses 1926-1931 (Vol. I-V)
Box 13
Series III. Interscholastic Competition (Vol. I of I)
Box 13
Series IV. Merit Solutions, Landscape Design 1912-1922 (Vol. 1-10)
Boxes 14,15
Series Va. Honors designs 1898/1899 - 1946/1947
Boxes 7-11, 16-23, 27-31
Series Vb. Honors designs 1947/1948 - 1951/1952 and 1952/1953 - 1956/1957
Boxes 24-26
Series VI. Design Problems, 1922/1923 - 1958/1959
Box 32
Series VII. Thesis Programs Series VIII. Other 1939-1951
Boxes 33,34
Series VIII. Other
Box 40
Series IX. Oversized Items
Mapcase
General
A partial guide of the project titles in the collection and names of their creators is available here, PDF file. A new series 1926-1927 starts on page 126 of the guide.
Cite As:
Cornell University College of Architecture, Art and Planning student projects, #15-5-1416. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Related Materials
Additional material and digital versions can be found online here: http://ecommons.library.cornell.edu/handle/1813/2938
Photographs. Architectural drawings. Ithaca (N.Y.) -- Buildings. Landscape architecture. City planning. Architecture -- Study and teaching. Cornell University. Department of City and Regional Planning Cornell University -- : Buildings.

Murdock192725" x 7" Envelopes Containing Drawings by Other Students19872"Problems in Mechanics" (Scrapbook) (AM W.drawings), Warren .P. Laird, Class in Mechanics, Cornell University1885-18863"Housing-Whose Responsibility?" (CRP Conference), Dept. of City and Regional Planning Publications19604"Cortland, NY General Plan"1954-19554"Johnson City, NY General Plan"1958-19594"Chemung County Urban Area"Fall 19644Dept. of City and Regional Planning Plans5
Scope and Contents
Includes Schohane Valley Townsite, Auburn General Plan4 Folders: Theses on Planning by Various Students, Including Raymond G. Ballard, Donald Beach, Allan Ray Cooper, and Arnold Nye6Miesuse: toward the mesoforms edited by Julián Varas.20066
Scope and Contents
Miesuse is a design research project developed in collaboration with a team of architecture students at Cornell University between January and May 2006. The project is part of an investigation on techniques of formal production in architecture, and it seeks to establish lines of continuity and transformation on the basis of spatial concepts developed by Mies van der Rohe during the period 1920-1945.Other student projects, course descriptions198035Other student projects, course descriptions198036Other student projects, course descriptions198037Other student projects, course descriptions198038Student work for the National Architectural Accrediting Board199739Lysander New City, a project for fourth and fifth year students197039"Praxis2" booklet200039Building design sketchesSingle folderSeries II. Scope and Contents
Miesuse is a design research project developed in collaboration with a team of architecture students at Cornell University between January and May 2006. The project is part of an investigation on techniques of formal production in architecture, and it seeks to establish lines of continuity and transformation on the basis of spatial concepts developed by Mies van der Rohe during the period 1920-1945.Other student projects, course descriptions198035Other student projects, course descriptions198036Other student projects, course descriptions198037Other student projects, course descriptions198038Student work for the National Architectural Accrediting Board199739Lysander New City, a project for fourth and fifth year students197039"Praxis2" booklet200039Building design sketchesSingle folderSeries II.
